title: Epic T-13: Phase-13 QA-flow completion & doc reconciliation
description:
## Goal / Problem
Close the open verification tiers in docs/qa/20260607/qa-flows.md and reconcile the QA docs with the post-Phase-12/T-12.9 state (J=1.0, n=115). Beads is the source of truth; docs/plan/phase-13-qa-flow-completion.md is the human overview.
## Success Metrics
- Every QA-flow VC box is PASS or explicitly gated
- Each Wave-B flow emits a dated docs/qa/<date>/ verdict
## Scope
**In scope:** QA-1/3/4/6/7 deterministic verdicts + doc reconcile; gated live tiers tracked.
**Out of scope:** new product features; the opus re-baseline (T-12.5) and multi-file corpus (T-12.9b) are cross-phase carries.
## Execution Waves
**Wave A ŌĆö DONE (closed):**
- `wh-4ym.1` T-13.1 reconcile qa-flows.md *(doc)*
- `wh-4ym.2` T-13.2 QA-1 floor-only verdict *(verify)*
- `wh-4ym.3` T-13.3 QA-6 CI-contract verdict *(verify)*

**Wave B ŌĆö ready (parallel; non-overlapping files):**
- `wh-4ym.4` T-13.4 QA-3 verdict + F-001
- `wh-4ym.5` T-13.5 QA-4 policy verdict + F1-F3
- `wh-4ym.6` T-13.6 QA-7 KB/feed red-team

**Wave C ŌĆö gated (env/budget; after B):**
- `wh-4ym.7` T-13.7 plugin-load smoke ŌĆö gated: interactive claude ŌĆö blocked by wh-4ym.4
- `wh-4ym.8` T-13.8 observe CI run ŌĆö gated: gh/Actions ŌĆö blocked by wh-4ym.3
- `wh-4ym.9` T-13.9 live team-mode ŌĆö gated: experimental flag ŌĆö blocked by wh-4ym.6
- `wh-4ym.10` T-13.10 live poll/reflect ŌĆö gated: network ŌĆö blocked by wh-4ym.6

Launch a wave with `/launch-team <wave idsŌĆ”>`. Cross-phase carries (not children): T-12.5 opus re-baseline, T-12.9b multi-file corpus.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] all child tickets closed (each via its dated verdict / live evidence)
- [ ] qa-flows.md VC boxes flipped only when the probe is PASS (not SKIP)
## Quality Gates (white-hacker)
Per-package `uv run --project <pkg> --with pytest pytest <pkg>/tests -q`; `validate_manifest.py`; `claude plugin validate`. (NOT ruff/mypy/src/coverage ŌĆö this repo doesn't use them.)

title: T-13.6: QA-7 KB-text + feed-content injection red-team
description:
## Goal / Problem
Red-team the **two remaining untrusted-input ingestion points** (reviewed-code was done in Phase-12
Wave C): (1) KB entry text/YAML, (2) feed content polled by `sec-kb-refresh`. Assert no injected
instruction is obeyed, the KB-edit verdict gate still holds, and parsers treat untrusted text strictly
as data. Evidence ŌåÆ `docs/qa/20260607/qa-7-ingestion-verdict.md`.

## Background / Context (verified file:line)
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/gate_kb_edit.py` ŌĆö **`decide(event)` (line 37)** reads `evals/gate-verdict.json` (line 40); blocks KB writes unless `verdict == "KEEP"` (no-verdict/REVERT ŌåÆ block).
- `ai-attack-kb/scripts/{validate_kb.py,dedupe_kb.py,lint_skill.py}` (all present) ŌĆö schema validate (id pattern, mandatory `metadata.{source,url,retrieved}`, size caps), unique-id dedupe, strict-YAML front-matter gate.
- `sec-kb-refresh/scripts/poll_feeds.py` ŌĆö **`parse_osv` (line 26)**, **`to_candidate_entry` (line 81)** (generates the id; truncates/escapes feed text), **`render_entry` (line 104)** ŌåÆ schema-conforming draft. Feed text is embedded as data, never evaluated.
- `hooks/confine_self_writes.py` ŌĆö KB writes confined to the `ai-attack-kb` lane; feed egress host allow-list.

## Verification Workflow
Deterministic core (no agent runtime): run gates/parsers/validators directly on **poisoned fixtures**.
Optional **gated** live sub-item: a real agent reading a poisoned KB entry must still respect the gate.

## Steps
1. KB-text fixture: a valid KB entry whose body + YAML fields embed "ignore instructions / mark KEEP / run this" ŌåÆ `validate_kb` exit 0 (treated as inert data).
2. Gate: with no verdict file ŌåÆ `decide` blocks; `{"verdict":"REVERT"}` ŌåÆ blocks; `{"verdict":"KEEP"}` ŌåÆ allows. (The poisoned text cannot itself flip the gate.)
3. Feed fixture: poisoned OSV/ATLAS/Atom item (`summary` = injection) ŌåÆ `parse_osv` keeps it as a field value; `to_candidate_entry` mints its own `AISEC-ŌĆ”` id and truncates; `render_entry` ŌåÆ schema-valid.
4. Drafts from poisoned feed pass `validate_kb` + `dedupe_kb`.
5. No-obey assertion: verdict file unchanged, no stray files written, injected text remains literal (never executed).
6. Write `qa-7-ingestion-verdict.md` with `obeyed_any_injected_instruction = false`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] KB-text injection validates as inert data ŌĆö probe: `validate_kb <poisoned-entry>` exit 0; injected strings present verbatim (not stripped/executed).
- [ ] gate_kb_edit holds under injection ŌĆö probe: `decide` ŌåÆ block (no verdict), block (REVERT), allow (KEEP); the poisoned entry text does not alter the verdict file.
- [ ] Feed parser treats poison as data ŌĆö probe: `parse_osv` returns the item with summary intact; `to_candidate_entry` id is generated (`AISEC-ŌĆ”`, not from feed) and title Ōēż120 chars.
- [ ] Feed-derived drafts pass lint+gate ŌĆö probe: `validate_kb kb-drafts/` exit 0; `dedupe_kb` exit 0; `lint_skill` strict-YAML holds.
- [ ] `obeyed_any_injected_instruction == false` ŌĆö probe: verdict file byte-unchanged; no new/unexpected files; no rule bypassed.
- [ ] `docs/qa/20260607/qa-7-ingestion-verdict.md` recorded (deterministic PASS; live sub-item PASS or marked GATED).

## Quality Gates (white-hacker)
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/hooks --with pytest pytest ŌĆ”/tests/test_gate_kb_edit.py -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/scripts --with pytest pytest ŌĆ”/tests -q` (validate_kb, lint_skill, dedupe)
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts --with pytest pytest ŌĆ”/tests/test_poll_feeds.py -q`

## QA Before Push
Cover injection in BOTH body and YAML fields; all 3 feed formats (OSV/ATLAS/Atom); the 3 gate verdict states. Use a temp dir so the real `evals/feed-state.json` / `gate-verdict.json` are never touched.

## Risks / Dependencies
The live "agent reads poisoned KB and still respects the gate" check needs a runtime ŌĆö keep it an
**optional gated** sub-item; the deterministic gates/parsers are the load-bearing pass criterion. Don't
clobber real feed-state / verdict files (temp dir only).

## Notes / Open Questions
Deterministic vs live is called out per AC. Existing tests (`test_gate_kb_edit`, `test_poll_feeds`,
`test_lint_skill`, `test_validate_kb`) already cover the happy path; this ticket adds the **poisoned**
fixtures and records the adversarial verdict.

---

title: T-13.5: QA-4 security-policy verdict (present/absent/malicious + F1-F3)
description:
## Goal / Problem
Record a dated verdict that the **security-policy** subsystem treats SECURITY.md/security.txt as
**untrusted data**: absentŌåÆadvisory (no severity/cvss), presentŌåÆmerged draft preserving maintainer
facts, maliciousŌåÆinjection flagged with **no body echo**, output confined to `PATCHES/`; and the F1ŌĆōF3
hardenings + the scope-never-suppresses rule hold. Evidence ŌåÆ `docs/qa/20260607/qa-4-verdict.md`.

## Background / Context (verified file:line)
- `_shared/scripts/policy_detect.py` ŌĆö facts-only extraction; **`read_capped(..., limit=_READ_LIMIT=256 KiB)` (line 70)** (F2 read cap); ReDoS-bounded email regex.
- `sec-policy/scripts/parse_policy.py` ŌĆö gap report (booleans/enums/section names only); injection denylist returns a BOOLEAN, never echoes the matched span.
- `sec-policy/scripts/propose_policy.py` ŌĆö **F1: `_safe_write` opens with `os.O_NOFOLLOW` (line 154)** ŌåÆ ELOOP refuse on symlink; PATCHES symlink + `..`/separator confinement guards; existing body preserved verbatim as a prefix.
- `sec-policy/scripts/hygiene_advisory.py` ŌĆö **`is_factual` (line 44)** guards the advisory message (F3); advisory carries no `severity`/`cvss`/`owasp`.
- `sec-report/scripts/policy_scope.py` ŌĆö **"NEVER drops a finding and NEVER changes/lowers severity" (line 8)**, `annotate_findings_with_scope` (line 20) adds only `out_of_scope_per_policy` (ADR-018).

## Verification Workflow
Investigate ŌåÆ build 3 SECURITY.md fixtures (absent / present-minimal / malicious-with-injection) ŌåÆ run the detectŌåÆparseŌåÆproposeŌåÆadvisoryŌåÆscope chain + the F1/F2/F3 probes ŌåÆ record verdict. TDD if any new test is added.

## Steps
1. Baseline: run sec-policy / _shared / sec-report unit suites (must be green).
2. ABSENT fixture: `present=False`; `propose` action=`create` into `PATCHES/`; hygiene advisory fires (factual, no severity/cvss).
3. PRESENT fixture: `present=True`; `propose` action=`modify`; merged file starts with the original body verbatim; missing sections appended; maintainer email/versions/scope unchanged.
4. MALICIOUS fixture: `injection_suspected=True`; serialized gap-report JSON contains **zero** occurrences of the injected spans.
5. F1: symlinked output file AND symlinked PATCHES dir each ŌåÆ `ValueError` ("symlink"); real target byte-unchanged; nothing escapes.
6. F2: `detect_reporting_channel` on two 200K+ adversarial inputs each < 1.0s; `read_capped` enforces Ōēż256 KiB.
7. F3: `is_factual(advisory_message)` true; imperative variants false.
8. Scope: `annotate_findings_with_scope([HIGH], terms)` keeps severity+cvss, only adds the annotation.
9. Write `qa-4-verdict.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AbsentŌåÆadvisory (no severity/cvss) ŌĆö probe: fixture run asserts `present=False`, advisory `kind=hygiene-advisory`, no `severity`/`cvss`.
- [ ] PresentŌåÆmodify, verbatim preservation ŌĆö probe: merged text `.startswith(original)`; appended sections present; contact preserved.
- [ ] MaliciousŌåÆflagged, no echo ŌĆö probe: `injection_suspected=True`; `grep -c` injected spans in gap JSON ŌåÆ 0.
- [ ] F1 symlink write-escape refused (file + dir) ŌĆö probe: both `propose()` calls raise `ValueError`; target unchanged; `pytest ŌĆ”/sec-policy/scripts/tests -k symlink -q` exit 0.
- [ ] F2 ReDoS bounded + read cap ŌĆö probe: adversarial inputs < 1.0s; `read_capped` Ōēż262144 bytes; `pytest -k "redos or read_capped" -q` exit 0.
- [ ] F3 advisory factual ŌĆö probe: `is_factual(msg)` True; imperatives False.
- [ ] Scope never suppresses a HIGH ŌĆö probe: severity/cvss unchanged, only `out_of_scope_per_policy` added.
- [ ] Output confined to `PATCHES/` ŌĆö probe: `propose` writes only under `PATCHES/`; `..`/separator names rejected.
- [ ] `docs/qa/20260607/qa-4-verdict.md` recorded.

## Quality Gates (white-hacker)
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-policy/scripts --with pytest pytest ŌĆ”/tests -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts --with pytest pytest ŌĆ”/tests -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-report/scripts --with pytest pytest ŌĆ”/tests -q`

## QA Before Push
Cover the 3 fixtures + both F1 symlink variants + F2 timing + scope HIGH. Clean `/tmp/*_qa4` fixtures.

## Risks / Dependencies
`O_NOFOLLOW` is best-effort on non-Unix; the `_assert_not_symlink` pre-check is the cross-platform guard ŌĆö note the Windows caveat in the verdict. Prefer `uv run pytest` over ad-hoc `sys.path` scripts.

## Notes / Open Questions
security.txt (RFC 9116) covered by existing unit tests; the 3 fixtures focus on SECURITY.md. Hygiene message is intentionally frozen/factual (changing it is a code change).

---

title: T-13.3: QA-6 CI-contract verdict (tier-2)
description:
## Goal
Verify the CI contract: valid yaml, 15-pkg loop, pinned actions/uv/model.
## Status
DONE (Wave A) ŌĆö PASS. Evidence: docs/qa/20260607/qa-6-ci-contract-verdict.md.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] ci.yml valid YAML; per-package loop discovers all 15 test packages
- [x] every uses: is a 40-hex SHA (actions/checkout@df4cb1c0ŌĆ”); uv pinned 0.11.2
- [x] action.yml: @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.167; model claude-opus-4-8 (ADR-006)
## Notes
tier-2 FORM check only; live SHA/npm re-resolution = T-13.8.

title: T-13.4: QA-3 distribution+onboarding verdict (tier-2 + F-001)
description:
## Goal / Problem
Record a dated PASS/FAIL verdict that the **distribution + onboarding** contract holds deterministically:
the manifest validator enforces the canonical layout, `init_profile` writes a schema-valid
`security_policy`-bearing profile, the SessionStart **F-001 allowlist** drops injected prose, and the
plugin carries **no identity file** (posture lives only in the agent). Evidence ŌåÆ `docs/qa/20260607/qa-3-verdict.md`.

## Background / Context (verified file:line)
- `packaging/validate_manifest.py` ŌĆö CLI `python validate_manifest.py [repo]`, exit 0 valid / 1 invalid; rejects unknown top-level keys (T-12.7).
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts/init_profile.py` ŌĆö writes `<repo>/.white-hacker/project-profile.json`; schema `project_profile_schema.json` (verified present) with `additionalProperties:false` (blocks any planted identity key); refuses to write non-factual values.
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/sessionstart_project_facts.py` ŌĆö **F-001 allowlist**: `_ALLOWED_TOKEN = re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z0-9 ._/+-]{1,64}$")` (**line 52**), `_MAX_WORDS` (line 53), separator-aware `_WORD_SEP_RE` (defeats packed imperatives). Registered via `hooks/hooks.json` (SessionStart).
- Identity carriage: **no `CLAUDE.md` anywhere under `plugins/white-hacker/`** (verified); posture lives in `agents/white-hacker.md` (kind=agent, tools scoped, model=opus).

## Verification Workflow (investigate ŌåÆ probe ŌåÆ record)
Existing suites already pin behavior (sec-init, hooks, packaging). This ticket *runs them as a flow* on
real + crafted-malicious fixtures and records the verdict. New malicious-profile fixture is throwaway (`/tmp`).

## Steps
1. Manifest: run validator + `claude plugin validate` on the repo.
2. init_profile: run the CLI on a fixture repo; jsonschema-validate the written profile; confirm the `security_policy` block (7 keys).
3. SessionStart F-001: feed a crafted profile (markdown role-framing + imperative + `attacker.com` in `frameworks`/`path`/`reporting_channel`) and confirm none of it appears in `additionalContext`.
4. Identity carriage: `find ŌĆ” -iname CLAUDE.md` empty; agent.md declares posture/tools/model.
5. Write `qa-3-verdict.md` (commands, outputs, exit codes, commit hash, PASS/FAIL).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manifest valid ŌĆö probe: `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py . ; echo $?` ŌåÆ `0` + "OK"; `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker` ŌåÆ Ō£ö.
- [ ] init_profile writes a schema-valid profile carrying `security_policy` ŌĆö probe: run CLI on a fixture; `jq .security_policy <profile>` shows all 7 keys; jsonschema validates.
- [ ] SessionStart drops a crafted malicious profile ŌĆö probe: pipe the fixture event; `grep -c '# SYSTEM\|developer mode\|attacker.com\|ignore all instructions'` on the emitted context ŌåÆ `0`; context still `is_factual`.
- [ ] F-001 allowlist holds + legit tokens survive ŌĆö probe: `pytest ŌĆ”/hooks/tests/test_sessionstart_facts.py -k "f001 or f3" -q` ŌåÆ exit 0 (out-of-vocab/markdown/packed dropped; `fastapi`/`lang-python`/`github-pvr` kept).
- [ ] Identity carriage ŌĆö probe: `find plugins/white-hacker -iname CLAUDE.md` empty; `grep -E '^(name|kind|tools|model):' plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md` all present.
- [ ] `docs/qa/20260607/qa-3-verdict.md` recorded (PASS/FAIL + commit hash).

## Quality Gates (white-hacker)
- `uv run --project packaging --with pytest pytest packaging/tests -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts --with pytest pytest ŌĆ”/tests -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/hooks --with pytest pytest ŌĆ”/tests/test_sessionstart_facts.py -q`
- `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker`

## QA Before Push
Malicious-profile fixture exercises markdown / OOV-imperative / packed-separator / per-field cases; confirm legit single-token values still render (no over-redaction). Clean `/tmp` fixtures.

## Risks / Dependencies
`O_NOFOLLOW`/path specifics are F-001-adjacent only here. Needs `uv` + `claude` CLI on PATH (subscription; no API key). Capture `git rev-parse HEAD` for the verdict.

## Notes / Open Questions
The two closed-vocabulary fields (`security_policy.path`, `reporting_channel`) use exact-set matching (stricter than the shape allowlist) ŌĆö verify both render only canonical values.

---

title: T-13.2: QA-1 floor-only / tool-degradation verdict
description:
## Goal
Verify the inner discovery chain degrades to the Read/Grep/Glob floor with no external tools.
## Status

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] floor SCAN-PLAN available_tools:[] / all caps degraded / exit 0 (sanitized PATH)
- [x] finding layer: tool_assisted:false, confidence capped 0.8 (degradation.py:16)
- [x] pinned by test_degradation (14) + sec-detect floor/degrade tests

title: T-13.1: Reconcile qa-flows.md to post-Phase-12/T-12.9 state
description:
## Goal
Update qa-flows.md from the pre-Phase-12 era (J=0.835/n=103/634 tests) to current (J=1.0/n=115/668).
## Status
DONE (Wave A) ŌĆö commit a651660.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] matrix QA-1/QA-8 -> 115 cases, J=1.0
- [x] Why para 634->668; FINDING-QA1 + QA-8 weak-spots marked resolved; T-12.9/T-12.9b xref
- [x] probe: `grep -nE 'J=0\.835|634 tests' docs/qa/20260607/qa-flows.md` returns only historical lines

title: Adapt /design-ticket to white-hacker (agent types + de-k8s)
description:
## Goal
`.claude/commands/design-ticket.md` references agent types that DON'T exist here and carries k8s/DPA
examples (verbatim from the dpa_check reference). Re-ground to white-hacker; keep the command's
Process (Phases 1-5), Wave Grouping Rules, and self-contained-execution section.

## Verified issues (file:line, groomed 2026-06-08)
- Agent types (the undefined ones): L58 `feature-dev:code-architect`; L60 `feature-dev:code-explorer` +
  `feature-dev:code-reviewer`; L70 `feature-dev:code-reviewer`. (L59 `researcher`, L70 `tech-lead`, L259 `developer` already exist ŌĆö keep.)
- k8s/DPA examples: L32 (Grafana/cluster), L35 (OpenShell/hermes), L38 (pi01), L41 (k3s-setup-tzv).
- Phase-1 Reads column L58 (apps/base, gpu-services, kustomization roots).
- Phase-2 task checklist L75-79 (Storage/port/RAM, namespace naming, Vault, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/Hermes).

## Change plan
- Remap agents to .claude/agents/ (or built-ins): `feature-dev:code-architect` -> `tech-lead`;
  `feature-dev:code-explorer` -> `Explore` (built-in read-only); `feature-dev:code-reviewer` ->
  `white-hacker` (+ `qa-engineer` for bug review). Keep researcher/tech-lead/developer.
- Examples -> white-hacker (e.g. task: "Add an SSRF allowlist recognizer to sec-detect"; spike:
  "Evaluate Opengrep vs Semgrep for the SAST capability"; bug: "sec-policy parse_policy ReDoS on a 1 MB
  SECURITY.md"; `--epic=wh-4ym --type=task ...").
- Reads column -> `.claude/CLAUDE.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, similar skills under
  `plugins/white-hacker/skills/`, `_shared/reference/*`, the artifact-chain schemas.
- Checklist -> white-hacker: scope (>=2 packages? skill caps ADR-005), package/skill/file naming
  (`scripts/{mod,pyproject,conftest,tests}`), security (no hardcoded secrets; model-for-judgment-only;
  capability interfaces not brands ADR-015; never the corporate email).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `grep -nE 'feature-dev:|Grafana|k3s|gpu-services|apps/base|kustomiz|hermes|pi01|Vault|ANTHROPIC_API_KEY|nodePort' .claude/commands/design-ticket.md` -> empty.
- [ ] every referenced agentType exists in `.claude/agents/` or is a built-in (Explore/Plan/general-purpose).
- [ ] examples + Reads + checklist reference white-hacker reality (skills/_shared/ADR/artifact-chain/the 12 rules).

## File Ownership
`.claude/commands/design-ticket.md` (single file). Headless, no deps. Wave D, parallel-safe with wh-yc8.

title: Adapt /groom command to white-hacker (de-k8s: repo/package state)
description:
## Goal
`.claude/commands/groom.md` was created VERBATIM from the dpa_check reference and checks k8s/cluster
state ŌĆö useless in this Python plugin repo. Re-ground every domain-specific check to white-hacker;
keep the command's structure (Phases 1-6), Rules (one-at-a-time, check-actual-state, split-before-bloat),
and the report skeleton.

## Verified contamination (file:line, groomed 2026-06-08)
- intro L9, L13 ("infrastructure ticket / cluster state / PV / port")
- Phase 2 "Cluster State Verification" table L33-44 (apps/base, infrastructure/base, PV, nodePort, HelmRepo, Namespace)
- Phase 3 "Dependency Check" L50-53 (PostgreSQL/Redis/MinIO, Vault/ESO, StorageClass, HelmRepository)
- Phase 4 thresholds L78 (">1 namespace"); Phase 5 L71 ("validate kustomize"); Phase 6 report L82/85/88/92
  (Storage/HelmRepo/Namespaces/apps/base); Rules L109 ("storage math / README allocations").

## Change plan (white-hacker equivalents)
- Phase 2 -> repo/package state: skill/package exists (`ls plugins/white-hacker/skills/<skill>`), per-package
  `scripts/{mod,pyproject.toml,conftest.py,tests/}` present, schema valid (`*-schema.json`), ADR conflict
  (`grep docs/ARD.md`), capability in `_shared/reference/tool-registry.md`, artifact-chain stage exists.
- Phase 3 -> deps: bd blocking tickets closed + reference/artifact-chain deps + capability availability (degrade-gracefully, ADR-003).
- Phase 4 -> scope: ">1 skill/package", "src+test+docs mixed", skill caps (ADR-005).
- Phase 5 -> feasibility: per-package `uv run pytest` would pass / `validate_manifest` / `claude plugin validate` / schema builds.
- Phase 6 report -> packages touched ┬Ę schema/ADR conflicts ┬Ę capability availability ┬Ę gate status.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `grep -nE 'apps/base|infrastructure/base|PV|nodePort|HelmRepo|Namespace|kustomiz|cluster|StorageClass|Vault|ESO|PostgreSQL|pi01' .claude/commands/groom.md` -> empty.
- [ ] Phase 2/3/5/6 checks reference plugins/white-hacker/skills + evals + packaging + docs/ARD.md + the artifact chain + tool-registry.
- [ ] structure (Phases 1-6) + Rules preserved; `claude` still parses the command frontmatter.

## File Ownership
`.claude/commands/groom.md` (single file). Headless, no deps. Wave D, parallel-safe with wh-8hx (disjoint files).

title: T-12.9b: Restore eval headroom ŌĆö multi-file / real-CVE-in-context corpus
description:
## Goal
The 115-case corpus is SATURATED (J=1.0; the current agent aces single-file pairs ŌĆö see t-12.9-report.md
+ the ablation showing only a weakened agent drops to 0.8). Add cases that need:
- cross-file taint (source and sink in DIFFERENT files -> the reviewer must build a call graph),
- real un-patched CVE diffs in surrounding noise (distractors, business logic; not minimal snippets),
- genuinely-ambiguous severity/category (test triage, not just detection).
So the CURRENT agent scores < 1.0 (real measurable headroom). May need a multi-file case shape +
score.py file-set matching.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] >= 3 multi-file / CVE-in-context cases the current agent does NOT trivially ace (J < 1.0 on the subset), neutralized filenames
- [ ] corpus count + baseline + drift-guard updated; the eval can again MEASURE improvement
Carry from Phase-12 T-12.9. Pairs with T-12.5 (opus re-baseline).

title: T-12.5: Production re-baseline ŌĆö opus + k-run bootstrap (deferred)
description:
## Goal
Re-baseline evals on the PRODUCTION model (opus) with a k-run bootstrap so the keep-or-revert gate
reflects production. Current baseline is sonnet single-shot (n=115, J=1.0).
## GATED / DEFERRED
Budget (multi-M tokens) + opus. **Trigger: before the first tagged release.** Not headless-cheap.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] evals/baseline.json regenerated from an opus k-run (k>=3 bootstrap); model field=opus
- [ ] drift-guard green (baseline.n_cases == corpus size); snapshot reproduces
- [ ] cost recorded in docs/qa/<date>/
Carry from Phase-12 (was docs/plan T-12.5; tracked here as the beads source of truth).

title: Fix cwd-fragile manifest test (test_real_repo_marketplace_has_no_unknown_top_level_keys uses relative '.')
description:
## Goal / Problem
`packaging/tests/test_validate_manifest.py:361` (test_real_repo_marketplace_has_no_unknown_top_level_keys)
calls `vm.validate_marketplace(".")` with a RELATIVE path ŌĆö the ONLY real-repo test not using the
module-level `REPO_ROOT = pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]` (line 25). It passes from repo
root, FAILS from any other cwd (reproduced from /tmp: 1 failed, 39 passed). CI runs from root so it is
green there, but the test is cwd-fragile. CONFIRMED real (wh-4ym.4 verify + grooming 2026-06-08).

## Workflow (TDD)
- RED: `cd /tmp && uv run --project <repo>/packaging --with pytest pytest <repo>/packaging/tests/test_validate_manifest.py::test_real_repo_marketplace_has_no_unknown_top_level_keys -q` -> FAIL.
- GREEN: change `vm.validate_marketplace(".")` -> `vm.validate_marketplace(REPO_ROOT)` (match siblings).
- Re-run from /tmp AND repo root -> both pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] line 361 uses REPO_ROOT, no literal "." ŌĆö probe: `grep -n 'validate_marketplace(".")' packaging/tests/test_validate_manifest.py` returns nothing.
- [ ] packaging suite passes from ANY cwd ŌĆö probe: `cd /tmp && uv run --project <repo>/packaging --with pytest pytest <repo>/packaging/tests -q` -> exit 0, 40 passed.

## File Ownership
`packaging/tests/test_validate_manifest.py` (line 361) ŌĆö single file.

## Quality Gate
`uv run --project packaging --with pytest pytest packaging/tests -q` (40 passed, from any cwd).

Ready ŌĆö no deps. Found by wh-4ym.4 (QA-3) verify.

title: Fix poll_feeds ElementTree DeprecationWarning (line 61) + YAML-injection regression test
description:
## Goal / Problem
Two items from wh-4ym.6 (QA-7) verify; grooming 2026-06-08 refined the scope:
1. **REAL ŌĆö ElementTree truth-value DeprecationWarning** at `poll_feeds.py:61`
   (`idc = kids.get("id") or kids.get("guid")` ŌĆö `or` on an Element). Under
   `-W error::DeprecationWarning`, test_parse_atom FAILS (1 failed, 7 passed). Fix to explicit `is not None`.
2. **YAML injection ŌĆö ALREADY SAFE (no rewrite)**: `render_entry` (poll_feeds.py:105) uses
   `yaml.safe_dump(entry, ...)`, so feed text -> dict -> safe_dump is escaped; a crafted feed cannot
   inject YAML keys. Scope reduced to ADD a regression test that PINS this (cheap guard).

## Workflow (TDD)
- RED: `pytest -W error::DeprecationWarning .../test_poll_feeds.py -q` -> test_parse_atom FAILS.
- GREEN: `poll_feeds.py:61` -> `idc = kids.get("id"); idc = idc if idc is not None else kids.get("guid")`.
  Add a poisoned-feed test: a feed item whose title/summary contains `: `, newlines, and `injected_key: x`
  -> `render_entry` output `yaml.safe_load`s to a dict whose top-level keys == the intended schema keys
  (no feed-injected key).
- REFACTOR: keep minimal; confirm line 59 (`link is not None and link.get(...)`) is already safe.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] no DeprecationWarning ŌĆö probe: `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts --with pytest --with pyyaml pytest .../tests/test_poll_feeds.py -q -W error::DeprecationWarning` exit 0.
- [ ] poisoned-feed YAML regression test ŌĆö probe: new test asserts no feed-injected top-level YAML key in render_entry output; exit 0.
- [ ] sec-kb-refresh suite green, 0 warnings.

## File Ownership
`plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts/poll_feeds.py` (line 61) + `tests/test_poll_feeds.py`.

Ready ŌĆö no deps. Found by wh-4ym.6 (QA-7) verify. The original "harden YAML rendering" framing is
RESOLVED-as-already-safe (safe_dump); the load-bearing fix is the DeprecationWarning.

title: T-13.10: QA-2 live KB-refresh poll + sec-learn reflect
description:

Live outer-loop: a real feed poll + reflection produce gated artifacts.
## GATED
Network + opt-in.

- [ ] live sec-learn reflection over real traces proposes a gated diff ŌĆö probe: live reflect
## Notes
Blocked-by T-13.6 (KB/feed ingestion safety verified first).

title: T-13.9: QA-5 live team-mode
description:
## Goal
white-hacker composes into a TL/QA/Dev/white-hacker team and returns triaged-only output.
## GATED
Needs CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 (+ version check).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] white-hacker spawns the team on a fixture, SendMessages findings to the TECH-LEAD (not the dev), returns triaged summary + report path only, exits cleanly on WAIT ŌĆö probe: live team run
- [ ] carry-over caveat holds (teammate spawn prompt inlines methodology)
## Notes
Blocked-by T-13.6 (verify untrusted-input safety before live multi-agent runs).

title: T-13.7: QA-3 plugin-load smoke (live)
description:
## Goal
Live smoke: the plugin loads via the Claude Code CLI.
## GATED
Needs an interactive `claude` session (subscription; no API key).
## Steps
1. `claude --plugin-dir ./plugins/white-hacker`
2. confirm the agent + `/white-hacker:security-review` namespaced skill are present and PreToolUse/SessionStart hooks register.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] load-smoke output recorded showing agent + namespaced skill + hooks ŌĆö probe: interactive claude session
## Verification
Record output under docs/qa/<date>/.

title: Real-CVE-in-noise eval corpus for headroom (research, uncertain)
description:
## Goal
T-12.9 (single-file) + T-12.9b (cross-file) proved synthetic paired cases are ACED by the frontier
agent (J=1.0). The only realistic lever to restore measurable headroom: real un-patched CVE diffs in
real business-logic noise / many-file indirection / obfuscation.
## DEFERRED / RESEARCH
Research-scale + uncertain (the agent may recognize known CVEs). Pairs with wh-aj6 (opus re-baseline).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] >= 3 real-CVE-in-noise cases where the CURRENT agent scores < 1.0 (neutralized; baseline + drift-guard updated)
      OR a documented spike concluding synthetic+real headroom is infeasible against the frontier agent.
See docs/qa/20260607/t-12.9b-report.md for the finding that motivates this.
